id: unique item identifier string; should be stable across dataset versions, e.g. 'presupposition-iterative-1'.
creation_method: how the item was obtained (corpus_mining / translation / generation).
phenomenon: main pragmatic phenomenon this item belongs to ('presupposition' / 'implicature' / 'information_structure').
utterance: the target utterance (e.g. a key phrase said by a speaker in the given context).

options.label: display label of the option (A / B / C / D).
options.type: internal option type identifier for the correct pragmatic inference, e.g. 'presupposition_true' or 'implicature_true'.
options.text: the full natural-language text of the answer option expressing the true intended pragmatic inference.

options.label: display label of the option (A / B / C / D).
options.type: internal option type identifier for a distractor option, e.g. 'distractor_lexical', 'distractor_associative', or another distractor subtype.
options.text: the full natural-language text of the distractor option.

options.label: display label of the option (A / B / C / D).
options.type: internal option type identifier for a distractor option, e.g. 'distractor_lexical', 'distractor_associative', or another distractor subtype.
options.text: the full natural-language text of the distractor option.

options.label: display label of the option (A / B / C / D).
options.type: internal option type identifier for a distractor option, e.g. 'distractor_lexical', 'distractor_associative', or another distractor subtype.
options.text: the full natural-language text of the distractor option.

correct_option: label of the correct displayed option, e.g. 'B'.

metadata.genre: genre or text type within the corpus (e.g. 'beletrie' / 'publicistika' / 'oborova literatura').
metadata.trigger: linguistic trigger or construction relevant for the item, e.g. 'opět', 'také', 'kdyby'.
metadata.negation: negation-related value or note if relevant for the item; otherwise an empty string.
metadata.note: optional annotator message or comment for the given item.
metadata.has_apposition: optional boolean field used only for possessive presupposition items; true if the referent is explicitly named.
